title: Bug X — Cursor hooks.json schema fix (version field + camelCase event names)
description: Rewrite the Cursor hooks.json template from the broken {events:{Stop,SessionStart,PreToolUse:[...]}} envelope to Cursor's official {version:1, hooks:{stop, sessionStart, preToolUse:[...]}} schema (verified against https://cursor.com/cn/docs/hooks). Top-level version is the literal number 1 (NOT string). Event names are camelCase (NOT PascalCase). Per-entry shape stays FLAT (Codex-style: {command, matcher?, type?, timeout?, loop_limit?, failClosed?}). Flip HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor in lockstep to keep array-append-with-dedupe semantics in deepMerge. Confirm extractFlatCommands is selected for the uninstall path (Cursor entries are flat — only top-level structure was wrong). Update integration test assertions, README citation, and add the version assertion as a schema lock. Zero migration shim — clean replace per memory feedback_clean_slate.md.

files.change: Replace {events: {Stop: [{command:'.cursor/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs'}], SessionStart: [{command:'.cursor/hooks/knowledge-hint-broad.cjs'}], PreToolUse: [{matcher:'Edit|Write|MultiEdit', command:'.cursor/hooks/knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs'}]}} with {version: 1, hooks: {stop: [{command:'.cursor/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs'}], sessionStart: [{command:'.cursor/hooks/knowledge-hint-broad.cjs'}], preToolUse: [{matcher:'Edit|Write|MultiEdit', command:'.cursor/hooks/knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs'}]}}. Version field MUST be number literal 1 (not string '1').

Change ['events.Stop', 'events.SessionStart', 'events.PreToolUse'] to ['hooks.stop', 'hooks.sessionStart', 'hooks.preToolUse'] — camelCase keys aligned with new template. Dotted paths MUST byte-match the template's actual top-level keys or array-append falls back to array-REPLACE silently (wipes user entries on re-install).

VERIFY (no edit expected) that extractFlatCommands is the extractor selected for Cursor at L232. Cursor entries are flat per-entry ({command} or {matcher, command}) — only the top-level envelope was wrong. If the call site uses extractFlatCommands, no edit needed; HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor change auto-propagates via shared import. If the call site hardcodes extractClaudeCommands, switch to extractFlatCommands.

files.target: L207-L221 (Cursor schema assertions in fresh-init test)
files.change: Rewrite assertions: (a) add expect(cursorHooks.version).toBe(1) as schema lock (number, not string); (b) replace cursorHooks.events.Stop/SessionStart/PreToolUse probes with cursorHooks.hooks.stop/sessionStart/preToolUse; (c) keep matcher 'Edit|Write|MultiEdit' assertion on preToolUse[0].matcher; (d) keep command path assertions ('.cursor/hooks/fabric-hint.cjs', '.cursor/hooks/knowledge-hint-broad.cjs', '.cursor/hooks/knowledge-hint-narrow.cjs').

files.target: L114-L131 exact triple-count + any Cursor-specific schema probes
files.change: AUDIT for Cursor-specific schema assertions (most assertions are filename-based, not schema-shape). Update only if a probe reads cursorHooks.events.X — change to cursorHooks.hooks.x (camelCase). Triple-count assertions on hook script files are unaffected because filenames are unchanged.

Remove 'Mirrors the Codex events.Stop[] envelope shape — Cursor's hook event vocabulary is not stable across releases' disclaimer. Replace with documentation of the new correct schema (top-level version:1 number + hooks:{stop,sessionStart,preToolUse} camelCase) citing https://cursor.com/cn/docs/hooks as authoritative reference.

implementation: Rewrite packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/cursor-hooks.json from {events:{Stop,SessionStart,PreToolUse:[...]}} to {version:1, hooks:{stop,sessionStart,preToolUse:[...]}} — version is number literal 1, event names camelCase, per-entry shape preserved (flat Codex-style with matcher for preToolUse only).

Edit packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts:138 — change HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor from ['events.Stop','events.SessionStart','events.PreToolUse'] to ['hooks.stop','hooks.sessionStart','hooks.preToolUse']. Lockstep with template top-level keys.

Run grep over packages/cli/src/install/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts around L222-L235 to confirm extractFlatCommands is selected for Cursor at L232 (expected — Cursor entries were always flat). Add/switch to extractFlatCommands if not.

Update packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts:207-221 — replace events.X assertions with hooks.x (camelCase), add expect(cursorHooks.version).toBe(1) as new schema lock assertion, preserve matcher + command-path assertions.
Audit packages/cli/__tests__/hooks-install-validate.test.ts for cursorHooks.events.X references; update to hooks.x (camelCase) if found. Triple-count filename assertions should be unaffected.
Rewrite packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/README.md L28-L34 Cursor section — remove 'assumed not verified' disclaimer, document correct schema, cite https://cursor.com/cn/docs/hooks.
Run pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test:typecheck && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli lint && pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test to verify all gates pass.

Manual smoke test: run `fab install` in a fresh tmpdir, inspect .cursor/hooks.json, manually verify against Cursor docs example. Optionally open the generated hooks.json in actual Cursor app to confirm acceptance.
Stage the 6 modified files + commit with message: 'fix(rc14): cursor hooks.json schema — version field + camelCase event names per official docs'.

reference.pattern: Template-fragment + deep-merge with arrayAppendPaths constants pattern (see skills-and-hooks.ts L135-L162 — single source of truth for paths shared between install and uninstall).
reference.examples: codex-hooks.json is the ANTI-pattern (events.X envelope correct for Codex but wrong for Cursor). claude-code.json shows the parallel pattern of top-level 'hooks' key (Claude-Code uses PascalCase + nested matcher envelope — Cursor's is camelCase + flat). json.ts deepMerge L58-L67 handles top-level scalar version:1 via primitive-REPLACE without engine changes.

criteria: .cursor/hooks.json after running `fab install` matches Cursor's official schema: top-level `version` is number literal 1, `hooks` is an object with camelCase keys `stop`, `sessionStart`, `preToolUse`

Integration test install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts asserts `expect(cursorHooks.version).toBe(1)` (number assertion, not toBe('1')) AND `cursorHooks.hooks.stop/sessionStart/preToolUse` keys exist with correct command paths
packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/README.md L28-L34 cites https://cursor.com/cn/docs/hooks and removes the 'assumed not verified' disclaimer
pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test passes 100% — no events.X references remain in test assertions
Manual Cursor-app test: generated hooks.json loads without 'Config version must be a number; Config hooks must be an object' error
Git commit created with subject 'fix(rc14): cursor hooks.json schema — version field + camelCase event names per official docs'

rationale.chosen_approach: Direct template rewrite + lockstep constant flip + test/README update. No migration shim, no schema validator, no version detection — clean-slate replace per memory feedback_clean_slate.md and the resolved clarification specifying version=1 hardcoded.
alternatives_considered: Add runtime schema validator (Zod/JSON Schema) as defense-in-depth — out of scope for stop-the-bleeding; defer to rc.15+
Detection-based version selection (read Cursor app version at install time, pick template version) — over-engineering
Preserve user's higher version on merge — adds custom merge logic; default deepMerge REPLACE semantics are correct per resolved clarification
decision_factors: Cursor schema fully resolved via docs fetch (no remaining uncertainty)
deepMerge engine already handles top-level version:1 via primitive-REPLACE (no engine changes)
Lockstep constraint: template top-level keys MUST byte-match HOOK_CONFIG_ARRAY_PATHS.cursor or array-append silently degrades to array-REPLACE
rationale.tradeoffs: Users with hand-edited .cursor/hooks.json from rc.13 get their version field overwritten on next install — acceptable because rc.13 schema was rejected by Cursor anyway, so no functional user state is being lost.

manual_checks: After running `fab install` in a clean tmpdir, open .cursor/hooks.json and verify top-level shape matches Cursor docs example byte-for-byte (modulo our specific command paths)
Open generated .cursor/hooks.json in Cursor app, verify no schema error in app logs/UI
Run `fab install` a second time in the same tmpdir, verify .cursor/hooks.json byte-identical to first run (no duplicates from array-append)
Verify README.md cursor section reads coherently and references official docs URL
success_metrics: 100% of cursor-schema assertions in install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts pass against new shape

0 references to 'events.Stop'|'events.SessionStart'|'events.PreToolUse' remain in packages/cli/templates/hooks/configs/cursor-hooks.json OR packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts.cursor constants OR test assertions
integration: packages/cli/__tests__/hooks-install-validate.test.ts (cross-client validate happy-path — should still pass unchanged)
